description: A transliterated Latin prayer (borrowed from a Christian thief-catching ordeal), an Arabic version of a gnostic saying of Secundus the Silent Philosopher, a Hebrew praise of God, an Aramaic recipe for path-jumping and the beginning of an Aramaic recipe (for revelation?) to be performed before the Torah-ark (קדם ארונא) of the synagogue. Information from GRU catalog via FGP and Bohak, "Catching a Thief," (2006).
description_he: טקסט פרא-ספרותי בלטינית. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
